parts.title: Mathematics Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] mathematics class that meets the curriculum goals for [specific focus, e.g. problem-solving], with three activities and an introduction.
Give me five strategies for motivating a [grade X] student who finds mathematics difficult, with one simple and encouraging idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class a basic mathematical skill (e.g. [specific skill, e.g. multiplication]), with three steps and one example.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. everyday mathematics] into a mathematics lesson for a [grade X] class, with three exercises for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases to build a [grade X] student's confidence during a mathematics task.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a mathematics lesson with a [grade X] class, with three steps and a brief number warm-up.
Suggest three ways to use everyday objects (e.g. [specific object, e.g. rulers]) in a mathematics lesson for a [grade X] class, with one activity for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute closing routine for a mathematics lesson with a [grade X] class, with three elements (e.g. reflection, summary).
Give me five ideas for making a mathematics lesson for a [grade X] class more memorable, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. geometry], with one game for each.
Create a list of three short reflection questions for a [grade X] class after a mathematics lesson on [specific theme, e.g. fractions], to capture their thoughts.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with technology teachers at a [middle school] to connect mathematics to [specific theme, e.g. measurements], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's understanding of [specific area, e.g. algebra], with three criteria.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. apps, games) for a mathematics lesson with a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. numeracy skills].
Suggest three ways to adapt a mathematics lesson on [fractions] for a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. dyscalculia], with one solution for each.
Create a template for a weekly mathematics plan for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. calculation, problem-solving, reasoning).
Give me five ideas for using minimal equipment in a mathematics lesson for a [grade X] class to explore [specific theme, e.g. area], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific mathematical concept, e.g. percentages], with one practical exercise for each.
Write a short guide for managing a disruptive mathematics lesson with a [grade X] class, with three steps and one calming strategy.
Give me an example of a mathematics lesson for a [grade X] class that combines mathematics with [specific theme, e.g. economics], with three elements.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with science teachers at a [compulsory school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. measurements] in mathematics, with one example for each.
Create a lesson for a [grade X] class where they solve a practical mathematics problem about [specific theme, e.g. budgeting], with three steps and one learning goal.
Give me five ways to make a theoretical unit in mathematics more engaging for a [grade X] class, with one creative idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class to solve [specific type of problem, e.g. equations], with three steps and one example.
Suggest three ways to connect a mathematics lesson for a [grade X] class to [specific theme, e.g. sustainability], with one exercise for each.
Create a list of five short practical exercises for a [grade X] mathematics class that reinforce [specific goal, e.g. multiplication], with a description for each.
Give me an example of a routine for closing a mathematics lesson with a [grade X] class using a short problem-solving task, with three steps.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools (e.g. [specific tool, e.g. GeoGebra]) in a mathematics lesson for a [grade X] class, with two tasks for each.
Write a short template for a 5-minute reflection after a mathematics lesson with a [grade X] class, with three questions about their reasoning.
Give me five ideas for integrating games into a mathematics lesson for a [grade X] class, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. addition], with one game for each.
Create a list of three short ways to support a [grade X] student who is struggling with [specific concept, e.g. fractions], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with Social Studies teachers at a [upper secondary school] to connect mathematics to [specific theme, e.g. statistics in society], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a [grade X] class to solve a geometry problem over one lesson, with three steps.
Write a list of five simple mathematics tasks for a [grade X] class that reinforce [specific goal, e.g. logical thinking], with a rationale for each.
Suggest three ways to engage a [grade X] class that is reluctant to participate in a mathematics lesson, with one method for each.
Create a template for a term plan for mathematics for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. number, geometry, problem-solving) and timeframes.
Give me five ideas for creating a simple mathematics exhibition with a [grade X] class, with minimal preparation, and one element for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific mathematical concept, e.g. probability], with one exercise for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. attention difficulties] in a mathematics lesson, with three steps.
Give me an example of a lesson for a [grade X] class that combines mathematics with [specific theme, e.g. technology], with three elements.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the student council at a [compulsory school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. mathematics in everyday life], with one example for each.
